method_atom: Bayesian parameter inference for an exponential-family probability model whose normalising constant (partition function) is analytically intractable, carried out with a Markov chain Monte Carlo scheme that circumvents evaluating that constant, and accelerated by a population/interacting-chains MCMC approach that improves convergence and mixing relative to Monte Carlo maximum-likelihood estimation.
object_atom: exponential random graph models — probabilistic models of network structure whose parameters weight configurations (edges, subgraph counts) of a graph.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is Bayesian/population-MCMC inference for a model with an intractable normalising constant; the domain-specific object is the exponential random graph model of network data.
